notes: This input covers only native source-lock components whose advisory source contract has no dedicated advisory index.
Terminal decisions require explicit CVE or named security-finding mapping to the locked source version plus two distinct approvals.
Current-release, recommended-release, and generic security-fix wording are not sufficient for terminal public-release vulnerability decisions.

evidence.summary: Apache Serf download metadata identifies 1.3.10 as the latest stable release and provides release integrity material, but it is not a versioned vulnerability advisory index.
ASF security process material is a reporting and CNA process source; it does not provide a Serf-specific terminal finding map for the locked version.

The Serf 1.3 changelog records Serf 1.3.7: Handle NUL bytes in fields of an X.509 certificate; NVD maps CVE-2014-3504 to Serf 0.2.0 through 1.3.x before 1.3.7, while the locked SubversionR Serf source is 1.3.10.
NVD records CVE-2014-3504 as affecting Serf 0.2.0 through 1.3.x before 1.3.7, while the locked SubversionR Serf source is 1.3.10.

terminalFindings.resolutionStatement: Serf 1.3.7 handled NUL bytes in fields of an X.509 certificate for the issue NVD records as affecting Serf 1.3.x before 1.3.7, and the locked static-link input source is Serf 1.3.10.

nonClaims: This record only permits the exact fixed decision for CVE-2014-3504 on Serf 1.3.10.
This record does not assert that Serf 1.3.10 is free of all known or unknown vulnerabilities.
This record does not claim public release readiness.

evidence.summary: APR security material is reporting guidance rather than a versioned public advisory index for APR 1.7.6.
ASF security process material does not provide a component-specific terminal finding map for APR 1.7.6.

The APR 1.7 changelog records Changes for APR 1.7.5 fixing CVE-2023-49582 and Changes for APR 1.7.1 fixing CVE-2022-24963, CVE-2022-28331, and CVE-2021-35940; the locked packaged runtime source is APR 1.7.6.
NVD records CVE-2023-49582 as affecting APR versions before 1.7.5, while the locked SubversionR APR source is 1.7.6.
NVD records CVE-2022-24963 as affecting APR 1.7.0, while the locked SubversionR APR source is 1.7.6.
NVD records CVE-2022-28331 as affecting APR 1.7.0 and earlier on Windows, while the locked SubversionR Windows APR source is 1.7.6.
NVD records CVE-2021-35940 as an APR 1.7.0 branch regression; the APR 1.7 changelog records the restore fix in APR 1.7.1 and the locked SubversionR APR source is 1.7.6.

terminalFindings.resolutionStatement: APR 1.7.5 fixed CVE-2023-49582, and the locked packaged runtime source is APR 1.7.6.
APR 1.7.1 fixed CVE-2022-24963, and the locked packaged runtime source is APR 1.7.6.
APR 1.7.1 fixed the Windows apr_socket_sendv() issue tracked as CVE-2022-28331, and the locked packaged runtime source is APR 1.7.6.
APR 1.7.1 restored the apr_time_exp*() fix tracked as CVE-2021-35940, and the locked packaged runtime source is APR 1.7.6.

nonClaims: This record only permits the exact fixed decision for CVE-2023-49582, CVE-2022-24963, CVE-2022-28331, and CVE-2021-35940 on APR 1.7.6.
This record does not assert that APR 1.7.6 is free of all known or unknown vulnerabilities.
This record does not claim public release readiness.

evidence.summary: APR-util shares the APR security reporting path, which is not a versioned public advisory index for APR-util 1.6.3.
ASF security process material does not provide a component-specific terminal finding map for APR-util 1.6.3.

The APR-util 1.6 changelog records Changes with APR-util 1.6.2 fixing CVE-2022-25147 and Changes with APR-util 1.6.1 including the SDBM corrupted database validation fix for CVE-2017-12618; the locked packaged runtime source is APR-util 1.6.3.
NVD records CVE-2022-25147 as affecting APR-util 1.6.1 and prior versions, while the locked SubversionR APR-util source is 1.6.3.
NVD records CVE-2017-12618 as affecting APR-util 1.6.0 and prior versions, while the locked SubversionR APR-util source is 1.6.3.

terminalFindings.resolutionStatement: APR-util 1.6.2 fixed CVE-2022-25147, and the locked packaged runtime source is APR-util 1.6.3.
APR-util 1.6.1 added the SDBM corrupted database validation fix tracked as CVE-2017-12618, and the locked packaged runtime source is APR-util 1.6.3.

nonClaims: This record only permits the exact fixed decision for CVE-2022-25147 and CVE-2017-12618 on APR-util 1.6.3.
This record does not assert that APR-util 1.6.3 is free of all known or unknown vulnerabilities.
This record does not claim public release readiness.

reviews.impactStatement: APR-ICONV-1.2.2-NO-PUBLISHED-ADVISORY is a source-review finding for APR-iconv 1.2.2 in SubversionR's win32-x64 packaged-runtime scope: the recorded Apache release metadata, APR-iconv changelog, GitHub advisory index, NVD keyword searches, and OSV queries identify no applicable published vulnerability for the locked packaged runtime. This does not assert that APR-iconv 1.2.2 is vulnerability-free.

evidence.summary: APR-iconv shares the APR security reporting path, so this terminal decision is based on explicit release, repository advisory, NVD, and OSV evidence instead of a dedicated project advisory feed.
ASF security process material remains the reporting path and does not itself assert APR-iconv vulnerability-free status.
Apache APR download metadata says APR iconv 1.2.2 is the best available version and that Windows APR-iconv is required to enable APR-util's apr_xlate feature.
APR-iconv 1.2.2 CHANGES records 'Win32: Resolve build issues with modern Visual Studio toolchains' and no CVE or security-fix entry for the locked 1.2.2 release.
The apache/apr-iconv GitHub Security Advisories page reports: There aren't any published security advisories.
Manual NVD keyword checks for apr-iconv, APR-iconv, apriconv, libapriconv, and Apache Portable Runtime Iconv returned totalResults 0 for each query on 2026-07-07.
Manual OSV /v1/query checks for the apache/apr-iconv Git repository and APR-iconv 1.2.2 package names returned empty vulnerability result objects on 2026-07-07.

terminalFindings.resolutionStatement: Manual review of Apache APR-iconv release metadata, APR-iconv 1.2 CHANGES, apache/apr-iconv GitHub advisories, NVD keyword searches, and OSV queries found no applicable published vulnerability for APR-iconv 1.2.2 in SubversionR's win32-x64 packaged-runtime scope; this does not assert that APR-iconv 1.2.2 is vulnerability-free.

nonClaims: This record only permits the exact not_affected decision for APR-ICONV-1.2.2-NO-PUBLISHED-ADVISORY on APR-iconv 1.2.2 in SubversionR's win32-x64 packaged-runtime scope.
This record does not assert that APR-iconv 1.2.2 is free of all known or unknown vulnerabilities.
This record does not claim public release readiness.

evidence.summary: Expat 2.8.1 release changes list CVE-2026-45186 as fixed in the locked version.
The project security overview is reporting guidance; the terminal grant is scoped to the CVE-2026-45186 release changelog evidence, not a broad clean-component claim.

terminalFindings.resolutionStatement: Expat 2.8.1 security fixes list CVE-2026-45186, and the locked packaged-runtime source version is 2.8.1.

nonClaims: This record only permits the exact fixed decision for CVE-2026-45186 on Expat 2.8.1.
This record does not assert that Expat 2.8.1 is free of all known or unknown vulnerabilities.
This record does not claim public release readiness.

reviews.impactStatement: CVE-2026-22184 is limited to zlib's standalone contrib/untgz demonstration utility. SubversionR uses zlib 1.3.2 as a static-link input to the packaged libsvn runtime and does not build or ship the untgz utility or contrib/untgz artifacts.

evidence.summary: zlib 1.3.2 release notes say the release addresses 7ASecurity audit findings, and the locked SubversionR source version is 1.3.2.
The project GitHub security overview does not provide a published advisory feed that can be treated as a versioned terminal decision source.
The zlib project issue for CVE-2026-22184 describes a global buffer overflow in TGZfname() under contrib/untgz/untgz.c, reached through the standalone untgz utility command-line argument.

NVD records CVE-2026-22184 as affecting zlib versions up to and including 1.3.1.2, limited to the standalone demonstration utility under contrib/untgz, and not affecting the core zlib compression library.

terminalFindings.resolutionStatement: NVD records CVE-2026-22184 as affecting zlib versions up to and including 1.3.1.2 and limited to contrib/untgz; SubversionR locks zlib 1.3.2 and does not build or ship the vulnerable untgz utility.

nonClaims: This record only permits the exact not_affected decision for CVE-2026-22184 on zlib 1.3.2 in SubversionR's packaged runtime scope.
This record does not assert that zlib 1.3.2 is free of all known or unknown vulnerabilities.
This record does not claim public release readiness.
